message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "2 runes + 1 moonstone yields 75G base premium (no block: different types)"
Expected: quote with 2 runes + 1 moonstone returns { premium: 110 } (3×25 base + 3×10 first insurance + 5 fee)
Current error: Expected { premium: 110 }, received { premium: NaN }
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Note: Need to add moonstone: 25 to BASE_PREMIUMS. Block does not apply because items are different types (existing isBlockOfThree should work — verify).

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal change needed.
I can see the issue. The `BASE_PREMIUMS` doesn't include `moonstone`, causing `NaN` when summing. I need to add `moonstone: 25` minimally.

content.text:
All 11 tests pass.

Verification of minimality:
- The `isBlockOfThree` function already correctly checks `items.every((item) => item.type === items[0].type)`, so it returns false for mixed types (2 runes + 1 moonstone). No change needed there.
- The only missing piece was the `moonstone` entry in `BASE_PREMIUMS`. Adding `moonstone: 25` is the minimal change needed: `2*25 (runes) + 1*25 (moonstone) + 3*10 (first insurance) + 5 (fee) = 110`.
- No future-test code added (e.g., I did not implement the "two separate blocks" logic for the next todo).

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added `moonstone: 25` to `BASE_PREMIUMS`.
- Result: All tests now pass (11 passing, 38 todo).
- Approach: Minimal — only added the missing item type entry. The existing `isBlockOfThree` correctly handles mixed types via the `every` check, so no block logic changes were needed.

Relevant file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_03-35-32_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
